# Vibe Flags — Full Documentation

> Offline-first feature flag toolkit that lives in your HTML. No servers. No config files. No build step. Made for agents and LLMs.

Vibe Flags is a set of web components for managing feature flags entirely in the browser. Add one CDN script tag, write HTML, and toggle features with a floating toolbar. State persists in localStorage.

---

## Getting Started

Add one script tag and start using feature flags in 60 seconds.

### Installation

CDN (recommended):
```html
<script type="module" src="https://unpkg.com/@vibe-flags/core"></script>
```

npm:
```bash
npm install @vibe-flags/core
```

### Quick Start

```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
  <script type="module" src="https://unpkg.com/@vibe-flags/core"></script>
</head>
<body>
  <h1>My App</h1>

  <vibe-flags-boolean name="showBanner" description="Welcome banner" value="true" default>
    <div style="padding: 16px; background: #dbeafe; border-radius: 8px;">
      Welcome to the beta! This banner is controlled by a feature flag.
    </div>
  </vibe-flags-boolean>

  <vibe-flags-toolbar></vibe-flags-toolbar>
</body>
</html>
```

### How It Works

1. The `<script>` tag loads Vibe Flags and registers the custom elements
2. Each flag component self-registers when it connects to the DOM
3. `<vibe-flags-toolbar>` discovers all registered flags and renders controls
4. Toggling a flag updates `localStorage` and re-evaluates every flag
5. Children stay hidden until JS confirms they should be visible — no flash of content

---

## Boolean Flags

`<vibe-flags-boolean>` is an on/off toggle that conditionally shows or hides content.

### Attributes

| Attribute | Type | Default | Description |
|-----------|------|---------|-------------|
| `name` | `string` | `''` | Unique flag identifier. Required. |
| `description` | `string` | `''` | Label shown in the toolbar. Falls back to `name`. |
| `value` | `string` | `''` | Expected value to show children. If omitted, children are always shown. |
| `default` | `boolean` | `false` | Initial value when no stored value exists. Also used by "Reset all to defaults". |

### Examples

Show when true (most common):
```html
<vibe-flags-boolean name="darkMode" description="Dark mode" value="true" default>
  <style>body { background: #1a1a1a; color: #fafafa; }</style>
</vibe-flags-boolean>
```

Show when false:
```html
<vibe-flags-boolean name="darkMode" value="false">
  <style>body { background: #fff; color: #111; }</style>
</vibe-flags-boolean>
```

Always show (registration only — omit `value`):
```html
<vibe-flags-boolean name="analytics" description="Analytics tracking">
  <script src="analytics.js"></script>
</vibe-flags-boolean>
```

Multiple elements, same flag:
```html
<vibe-flags-boolean name="darkMode" value="true">
  <div class="dark-indicator">Dark mode active</div>
</vibe-flags-boolean>

<vibe-flags-boolean name="darkMode" value="false">
  <div class="light-indicator">Light mode active</div>
</vibe-flags-boolean>
```

---

## Select Flags

`<vibe-flags-select>` lets users choose between multiple options. Each option is a `<vibe-flags-option>` child.

### `<vibe-flags-select>` Attributes

| Attribute | Type | Default | Description |
|-----------|------|---------|-------------|
| `name` | `string` | `''` | Unique flag identifier. Required. |
| `description` | `string` | `''` | Label shown in the toolbar. Falls back to `name`. |
| `default` | `string` | first option | Initial option value. Must match a child option's `value`. |
| `custom` | `boolean` | `false` | Adds a "Custom..." option with free-text input in the toolbar. |

### `<vibe-flags-option>` Attributes

| Attribute | Type | Default | Description |
|-----------|------|---------|-------------|
| `value` | `string` | `''` | The option identifier. Children shown only when active. |

### Examples

Basic select:
```html
<vibe-flags-select name="theme" description="Color theme">
  <vibe-flags-option value="light">
    <style>body { background: #fff; color: #111; }</style>
  </vibe-flags-option>
  <vibe-flags-option value="dark">
    <style>body { background: #111; color: #fff; }</style>
  </vibe-flags-option>
</vibe-flags-select>
```

With explicit default:
```html
<vibe-flags-select name="theme" description="Theme" default="dark">
  <vibe-flags-option value="light"><p>Light</p></vibe-flags-option>
  <vibe-flags-option value="dark"><p>Dark</p></vibe-flags-option>
</vibe-flags-select>
```

With custom free-text input:
```html
<vibe-flags-select name="api-url" description="API URL" custom>
  <vibe-flags-option value="https://api.example.com"><p>Production</p></vibe-flags-option>
  <vibe-flags-option value="https://staging.example.com"><p>Staging</p></vibe-flags-option>
</vibe-flags-select>
```

A/B testing UI variants:
```html
<vibe-flags-select name="hero" description="Hero section" default="minimal">
  <vibe-flags-option value="minimal">
    <section><h1>Simple and clean</h1></section>
  </vibe-flags-option>
  <vibe-flags-option value="bold">
    <section style="background: #111; color: #fff;">
      <h1>Big and bold</h1>
    </section>
  </vibe-flags-option>
</vibe-flags-select>
```

---

## Toolbar

`<vibe-flags-toolbar>` renders a floating action button that opens a slide-out sidebar with controls for all registered flags.

### Attributes

| Attribute | Type | Default | Description |
|-----------|------|---------|-------------|
| `position` | `string` | `'bottom-right'` | Initial corner. One of: `bottom-right`, `bottom-left`, `top-right`, `top-left`. Overridden by saved user preference. |

### Behavior

- The FAB is draggable — drag to any corner and it snaps. Position saved in localStorage (`vibe-flags:toolbar-position`).
- The panel is resizable by dragging the edge handle. Size saved in localStorage (`vibe-flags:toolbar-size`).
- Uses Shadow DOM with `all: initial` — global page styles cannot leak into the toolbar.
- "Reset all to defaults" button resets every flag to its `default` attribute value.

```html
<vibe-flags-toolbar></vibe-flags-toolbar>
<vibe-flags-toolbar position="top-left"></vibe-flags-toolbar>
```

---

## URL Query Parameter Overrides

Override any flag's value via URL without touching localStorage.

Format: `?vf:<flag-name>=<value>`

```
https://yourapp.com?vf:darkMode=true&vf:theme=dark
```

Rules:
- URL params take priority over localStorage values
- URL params are ephemeral — not written to localStorage, don't persist across page loads
- Boolean flags accept `true` or `false` only; other values are ignored
- Select flags accept any value matching a registered option; unrecognized values are ignored

---

## Store API

The `vibeFlagsStore` provides an imperative JavaScript API for managing flags programmatically.

```ts
import { vibeFlagsStore } from '@vibe-flags/core';
```

### Methods

**`register(config)`** — Register a single flag. Reads existing value from localStorage.
```ts
vibeFlagsStore.register({ key: 'beta', type: 'boolean', label: 'Beta Mode' });
vibeFlagsStore.register({ key: 'theme', type: 'select', options: ['light', 'dark'], label: 'Theme' });
```

**`unregister(key)`** — Remove a flag from the store.
```ts
vibeFlagsStore.unregister('beta');
```

**`get(key)`** — Get current value. Returns `undefined` if not registered.
```ts
vibeFlagsStore.get('beta');  // false
vibeFlagsStore.get('theme'); // 'light'
```

**`set(key, value)`** — Set a flag value. Validates, persists to localStorage, dispatches event. Invalid values silently ignored.
```ts
vibeFlagsStore.set('beta', true);
vibeFlagsStore.set('theme', 'dark');
```

**`getAll()`** — Get all flag values as a plain object.
```ts
vibeFlagsStore.getAll(); // { beta: false, theme: 'light' }
```

**`getConfig()`** — Get all registered flag configurations.

**`getConfigForKey(key)`** — Get config for a specific flag.

**`reset()`** — Reset all flags to defaults and clear localStorage.

### Events

**`vibe-flags-changed`** — Dispatched on `window` when any flag changes.
```ts
window.addEventListener('vibe-flags-changed', (e) => {
  const { key, state } = e.detail;
  console.log(`Flag "${key}" changed`, state);
});
```

| Property | Type | Description |
|----------|------|-------------|
| `detail.key` | `string \| undefined` | Flag that changed, or `undefined` for reset |
| `detail.state` | `VibeFlagsState` | Snapshot of all current values |

---

## TypeScript Types

All types exported from `@vibe-flags/core`:

```ts
interface VibeFlagsConfigBoolean {
  key: string;
  type: 'boolean';
  label?: string;
  default?: boolean;
}

interface VibeFlagsConfigSelect {
  key: string;
  type: 'select';
  options: string[];
  label?: string;
  default?: string;
}

type VibeFlagsConfig = VibeFlagsConfigBoolean | VibeFlagsConfigSelect;
type VibeFlagsValue = boolean | string;

interface VibeFlagsState {
  [key: string]: VibeFlagsValue;
}
```

---

## React Integration

`@vibe-flags/core` includes React utilities under `@vibe-flags/core/react`. React 18 or 19 required.

### Components

**`VibeFlagsBoolean`** — Wraps `<vibe-flags-boolean>`.
Props: `name` (required), `description`, `value`, `default` (boolean), `children`.

```tsx
import { VibeFlagsBoolean } from '@vibe-flags/core/react';

<VibeFlagsBoolean name="darkMode" description="Dark Mode" default={false}>
  <style>{`body { background: #1a1a1a; color: #fff; }`}</style>
</VibeFlagsBoolean>
```

**`VibeFlagsSelect`** — Wraps `<vibe-flags-select>`.
Props: `name` (required), `description`, `default`, `children`.

**`VibeFlagsOption`** — Wraps `<vibe-flags-option>`.
Props: `value` (required), `children`.

```tsx
import { VibeFlagsSelect, VibeFlagsOption } from '@vibe-flags/core/react';

<VibeFlagsSelect name="theme" default="light">
  <VibeFlagsOption value="light"><LightTheme /></VibeFlagsOption>
  <VibeFlagsOption value="dark"><DarkTheme /></VibeFlagsOption>
</VibeFlagsSelect>
```

**`VibeFlagsToolbar`** — Renders the floating toolbar. No props required.

### Hook: `useVibeFlags`

```ts
useVibeFlags(key: string): VibeFlagsValue | undefined
useVibeFlags(config: VibeFlagsConfig): VibeFlagsValue
```

Subscribe by key (flag must be registered elsewhere):
```tsx
const enabled = useVibeFlags('featureX');
```

Register and subscribe inline:
```tsx
const darkMode = useVibeFlags({ key: 'darkMode', type: 'boolean', default: false });
const theme = useVibeFlags({ key: 'theme', type: 'select', options: ['light', 'dark'], default: 'light' });
```

### Imperative Registration

```tsx
import { vibeFlagsStore } from '@vibe-flags/core';

vibeFlagsStore.register({ key: 'darkMode', type: 'boolean', default: false });
vibeFlagsStore.register({ key: 'theme', type: 'select', options: ['light', 'dark', 'auto'], default: 'light' });
```

---

## AI Agent Integration

Vibe Flags is designed to be readable and writable by AI. Flags are plain HTML — no SDK to learn.

### For humans — paste into your AI assistant:
```
Design 5 distinctly unique and visually different variations of a section
of my website. Use Vibe Flags (https://vibe-flags.appwrite.network/llms-full.txt)
to let me switch between variants in its toolbar.

Section to redesign: [EXPLAIN YOUR SECTION HERE]
```

### For AI agents — add to system prompt or CLAUDE.md:
```
When designing UI components, generate multiple distinct variants and use
Vibe Flags (https://vibe-flags.appwrite.network/llms-full.txt) to let
the user switch between them in the browser.
```

---

- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/@vibe-flags/core
